desc: Command: Exports all room boundaries to JSON files for AutoCAD import.
summary: Command: Exports all room boundaries to JSON files for AutoCAD import. Creates one JSON file per level in the same folder as the Revit model. WORKFLOW: 1. User picks a base point (export origin) 2. Command retrieves all rooms from host + linked models 3. Creates ZoneDataStorage for each room with boundary coordinates 4. Uses ContextZones to filter and format export data 5. Exports one JSON file per level to model folder
usecase: User wants to export room boundaries for use in AutoCAD. User needs zone layouts for 2D coordination drawings. User wants to transfer spatial data between Revit and AutoCAD.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open and saved (for export path). At least one room must exist in the model or linked models.
postcondition: JSON files created in the Revit model folder, one per level. Files named: Zones_{LevelName}.json Coordinates are in mm, relative to the selected base point.
sideeffects: Creates JSON files in the model folder. Does not modify any Revit elements.
logictree: check: document.isSaved → continue, else → error (need save location)action: promptUserToPickBasePoint → getBasePointaction: getAllRoomsIncludingLinks → SearchInsideCurrentModel.GetAllRoomsIncludingLinkscheck: rooms.count > 0 → continue, else → error (no rooms found)loop: foreach room → create ZoneDataStorageaction: groupByLevel → ContextZones.CreateExportModelsForAllLevelsloop: foreach level → saveJsonFileresolve: success → showSuccess, else → showError
